sample_rows.Decision Locked: Recovery owns ALL deviations from intended coordination outcome.
sample_rows.Rationale: Resolves Coord vs Recovery boundary. Failure events ALWAYS belong to Recovery regardless of where they originated. Originating context emits a fact; Recovery owns the deviation lifecycle.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: Session owns capacity. Booking owns financial commitment. Seat allocation MUST NOT depend synchronously on payment finalization.
sample_rows.Rationale: Prevents deadlocks. Session reserves a seat (ephemeral hold); Booking confirms it (financial commitment). Two-phase, async between.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: Policies are stateless, do not orchestrate, do not call external systems, only evaluate and emit decisions.
sample_rows.Rationale: Prevents Policy Layer from becoming a god layer.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: TrustScore = f(use_case, skill, reliability, financial, community). Use-case binding is mandatory.
sample_rows.Rationale: No service computes 'reputation' generically. Every consumer declares its use case. Different combinations for matchmaking, GameTime gating, review display.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: Host PROVIDES capabilities. Coordination OWNS game assembly. Host is never an orchestrator.
sample_rows.Rationale: Prevents Host from becoming a hidden Coordination layer.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: Inventory = physical truth. Partner Relations = contractual truth. Availability ≠ entitlement. Pricing ≠ negotiation terms.
sample_rows.Rationale: Two contexts, two kinds of truth. Never mixed.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: Build order: Events → Aggregates → Policies → Workflows. NEVER the reverse.
sample_rows.Rationale: Forces fact-first thinking. Aggregates exist to emit events; policies exist to consume them.

sample_rows.Decision Locked: Each sheet maps to ONE layer: Domain Facts | Aggregates | Policies | Workflows | ACLs. No mixing.
sample_rows.Rationale: Physical layer separation in the document mirrors logical separation in the system.

header_names: Trigger (what just happened in the world)

sample_rows.Payload: gameId, proposerId, sport, timeWindow, location, skillRange, capacity
sample_rows.Trigger (what just happened in the world: A user expressed intent to play (sport, time window, location, skill range)
Game stopped accepting joiners (full or by host)
Game expired without ever scheduling a Session

sample_rows.Payload: sessionId, gameId, venueId, courtId, timeSlotId, capacity, scheduledAt
sample_rows.Trigger (what just happened in the world: A Session was scheduled against a Game (venue + slot locked)
A seat in a Session is reserved for a player (ephemeral hold, TTL)
A SeatHold was upgraded to confirmed (Booking is paid)
A SeatHold was released (TTL expired or explicit)
Session reached min participants and is locked in
Session cancelled before play (insufficient seats / host-cancelled / venue-cancelled)

sample_rows.Invariants: Status monotonic: Proposed→Open→Closed→(Scheduled|Abandoned). Capacity immutable after Open. Game does NOT own seats — only intent.
sample_rows.Owns: gameId, proposerId, sport, timeWindow, location, skillRange, capacity, status

sample_rows.Emits: SessionScheduled, SeatHeld, SeatReleased, SeatConfirmed, SessionConfirmed, SessionCancelled
sample_rows.Invariants: confirmedSeats + heldSeats ≤ capacity. Status monotonic: Scheduled→Confirmed→Started→Completed | Cancelled. Session OWNS capacity. Seat allocation does NOT depend synchronously on payment (L2).
sample_rows.Owns: sessionId, gameId(ref), venueId(ref), courtId(ref), timeSlotId(ref), capacity, confirmedSeats(count), heldSeats(count), status, version

sample_rows.Invariants: One Booking per (sessionId, userId). Status monotonic: Created→Confirmed | Cancelled | Refunded. Booking OWNS financial commitment. Booking does NOT own seat — only the financial side of one (L2).
sample_rows.Owns: bookingId, sessionId(ref), userId(ref), amount, snapshotId(ref), paymentId(ref?), status

sample_rows.Invariants: Immutable after MatchCompleted. Source of truth for post-event facts.
sample_rows.Owns: matchId, sessionId(ref), startedAt, completedAt, attendedUserIds, noShowUserIds

sample_rows.Emits: PlayerCancelled, HostCancelled, VenueCancelled (consumed), refund/replacement decisions emitted
sample_rows.Invariants: One case per cancellation event. Status monotonic. Owns the deviation lifecycle.
sample_rows.Owns: caseId, sessionId(ref) | bookingId(ref), initiatorType (Player|Host|Venue|System), reason, openedAt, resolvedAt, resolution

sample_rows.Invariants: Immutable detection. Penalty application is delegated to ReliabilityPenaltyPolicy.
sample_rows.Owns: caseId, sessionId(ref), userId(ref), matchId(ref), detectedAt, penaltyApplied?

sample_rows.Invariants: Lifecycle: Searching→Found|Failed. Searching has TTL based on time-to-session-start.
heldCount + confirmedCount ≤ capacity. NEVER violated. Counter-based, not list-based. PHYSICAL TRUTH ONLY (L6).
sample_rows.Owns: timeSlotId, courtId(ref), venueId(ref), date, startTime, endTime, capacity, heldCount, confirmedCount, version

sample_rows.Invariants: ≥1 Court to be bookable. Operating hours constrain TimeSlot generation. PHYSICAL TRUTH ONLY.
sample_rows.Owns: venueId, partnerId(ref), name, address, geo, courts(ids only), operatingHours, sportsSupported

sample_rows.Forbidden Behavior: MUST NOT call PG. MUST NOT mutate aggregates. Emits a SubsidyDecisionMade fact only.
sample_rows.Inputs: Cell projection (recomputed), historical fill rates, capital budget

sample_rows.Forbidden Behavior: MUST NOT manipulate trust signals. MUST NOT push notifications directly (delegates to NotificationsACL).
MUST NOT compute a generic 'reputation score'. MUST bind to a use_case (L4). Different combinations per use case.
sample_rows.Output (Decision): Use-case-specific decision (e.g., Eligible|Ineligible for matchmaking; Allow|Deny|RequireDeposit for gating)
sample_rows.Use Cases: Per use case: matchmaking | GameTime gating | display | dispute resolution

sample_rows.Forbidden Behavior: MUST NOT directly write to ReliabilityProfile — emits an observation that the profile consumes.
MUST NOT execute refund. Emits a RefundDecision; Financial context executes.
sample_rows.Inputs: BookingCancelled | SessionCancelled, cancellation timing, reason, FinancialTrustProfile

sample_rows.Forbidden Behavior: MUST NOT influence Trust composition. Karma stays in Gamification.
MUST be deterministic given inputs. MUST NOT seal snapshots itself — emits computation that PriceSnapshot aggregate seals.
sample_rows.Inputs: TimeSlot, time-of-day, demand signals, applicable discounts, GST tier

sample_rows.Forbidden Behavior: MUST NOT contact users directly. Emits candidate list.
sample_rows.Inputs: ReplacementCase, time-to-session, skill range, eligible pool

sample_rows.Forbidden Behavior: MUST NOT grant authority itself. Emits qualification fact; HostProfile aggregate decides.
sample_rows.Inputs: User activity, completion history, reliability profile

sample_rows.Compensation: If TimeSlotHold fails: GameAbandoned. If PriceSnapshot fails: TimeSlotReleased + GameAbandoned.
sample_rows.Steps (forward): 1. Game accepts joiners. 2. When threshold met, request TimeSlotHeld from Inventory. 3. Request PriceSnapshotSealed from Pricing. 4. Emit SessionScheduled.

sample_rows.Compensation: PaymentFailed → BookingCancelled → SeatReleased. Reservation TTL backstop.
sample_rows.Steps (forward): 1. Create Booking. 2. PaymentAuthorized. 3. PaymentCaptured. 4. BookingConfirmed. 5. SeatConfirmed.

sample_rows.Compensation: Refund failures retry with backoff; manual escalation if exhausted.
sample_rows.Steps (forward): 1. Open CancellationCase. 2. Apply RefundEligibilityPolicy. 3. RefundIssued. 4. SeatReleased. 5. If host/venue cancel: cascade to all bookings.

sample_rows.Compensation: If no replacement found and time runs out: ReplacementCase Failed; Recovery decides downstream.
sample_rows.Steps (forward): 1. Open ReplacementCase. 2. Apply ReplacementSearchPolicy. 3. Notify candidates. 4. ReplacementFound or expire.
1. Compare attendedUserIds vs confirmedUserIds. 2. For each missing: open NoShowCase. 3. Apply ReliabilityPenaltyPolicy.

sample_rows.Compensation: If subsidy fails: log and feed back to model retraining.
sample_rows.Steps (forward): 1. Cell projection computed. 2. SubsidyDecisionPolicy evaluated. 3. If Subsidize: emit SubsidyDecisionMade. 4. Game-to-Session Saga starts with system as host.

sample_rows.Translation Direction: External order/intent ↔ Internal Booking, internal Payment ↔ external transaction
sample_rows.What it Forbids: PG-specific concepts (e.g. 'order') from leaking into Coordination context.
Maps API types from appearing in Inventory aggregates.
Direct provider calls from Coordination, Recovery, or any policy.
Provider-specific session models from leaking into User aggregate.
PMS-specific data structures from polluting Inventory.

sample_rows.Purpose: Demand cluster definition for liquidity policies (geography, time, sport, skill band)
sample_rows.Why NOT an Aggregate: No invariants. No transactional consistency. Recomputed continuously. Demoted from v5 aggregate.
Predictive model output. Versioned model, not state.
Cache. Authoritative state lives in 4 trust profiles.
Karma ledger is authoritative; ranking is derived.

sample_rows.Detection Signal in Code: Method signatures lacking use_case parameter; persistence schemas containing composed/overall/total trust columns; tests that depend on a precomputed reputation value.
sample_rows.Hard Rules:
(a) NO persisted composed_score anywhere — no column, no document, no row.
(b) NO aggregate or table representing composed trust.
(c) Trust Composition Policy is a pure, stateless, versioned function.
(d) Caches allowed for performance ONLY, never for correctness.
(e) NO downstream system may depend on a cached composed value for decision correctness — cache miss MUST NOT change behavior.
(f) Every call to Trust Composition MUST pass a use_case parameter.
sample_rows.Protects: Trust submodel separation. Prevents 4 trust profiles from being collapsed into a single composed score under cache/optimization pressure.
sample_rows.Structural Test (when violated): If you find: (i) a cache key shaped like 'reputation:{userId}' without use_case; (ii) any column named composed_score / overall_trust / reputation; (iii) a method signature getReputation(userId) without use_case; (iv) a service that breaks when the composed cache is empty.

sample_rows.Detection Signal in Code: Method signatures like host.approveJoinerForSession(session, user); HostProfile importing Coordination types; sagas with Host as a step actor.
sample_rows.Hard Rules:
(a) Host emits ONLY capability events (HostQualified, HostAuthorityGranted) and obligation/failure events (HostObligationFailed).
(b) Host MUST NOT mutate coordination state (Game, Session, Booking, Match).
(c) Host MUST NOT trigger workflows.
(d) Host MUST NOT hold references to coordination aggregates.
(e) Host CANNOT be part of any saga orchestration step as a decision-making actor.
(f) No method on HostProfile may take a Session, Game, Booking, or Match as a parameter.
sample_rows.Protects: Hosting as capability provider only. Prevents Host from becoming a hidden Coordination orchestrator.
sample_rows.Structural Test (when violated): If Host appears in a saga as a decision step (not just as a capability check); if HostProfile has any method touching coordination types; if a workflow is initiated by a Host event other than capability/obligation events.

sample_rows.Detection Signal in Code: Policy classes with injected repositories or HTTP clients; policy methods with void return types; policy code calling event publishers.
sample_rows.Hard Rules:
(a) Policies MUST NOT call external systems (PG, DB writes, third-party APIs).
(b) Policies MUST NOT orchestrate workflows.
(c) Policies MUST NOT emit domain events directly — they return decisions, the caller emits the event.
(d) Policies MUST NOT store state.
(e) Policies CONSUME facts and RETURN decisions. Nothing else.
sample_rows.Protects: Policy layer non-aggregation. Prevents Policy Layer from becoming a god layer.
sample_rows.Structural Test (when violated): If a policy method has side effects (writes, network calls, state mutation); if a policy has dependencies beyond its input data (e.g., a DB connection); if a policy emits an event itself instead of returning a decision the caller emits.

sample_rows.Detection Signal in Code: Coordination aggregates with cancellation_reason fields; Coordination methods named computeRefund() or applyPenalty(); Recovery code that updates Match.attendedUserIds.
sample_rows.Hard Rules:
(a) ALL deviation events are owned by Recovery.
(b) Coordination MUST NOT contain cancellation branching logic.
(c) Coordination MUST NOT compute penalties.
(d) Coordination MUST NOT trigger refunds.
(e) Recovery MUST NOT redefine original intent.
(f) Recovery MUST NOT mutate coordination history (Match is immutable).
sample_rows.Protects: Recovery as core subdomain owns ALL deviations. Prevents Coordination from sneaking in cancellation/penalty/refund logic.
sample_rows.Structural Test (when violated): If Coordination has if-cancelled branches in its main logic; if Coordination computes any penalty or refund amount; if Recovery writes back to Match or rewrites a Booking's original amount.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Class name: Game. Methods touch only intent attributes. NO seat/money/attendance methods.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: Pre-commitment INTENT to play. Has sport, time window, location, skill range, capacity. Does NOT own seats or money.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a Session. Not a Booking. Not a Match.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Class name: Session. Owns capacity counters. Owns SeatHeld/SeatConfirmed/SeatReleased.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: Scheduled INSTANCE of a Game (venue + time slot locked). Owns CAPACITY via heldCount/confirmedCount. Mutable roster, immutable schedule.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a Game (which is intent). Not a Booking (which is the financial side). Not a Match (which is post-event).

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Class name: Booking. References sessionId and paymentId. NEVER owns seat allocation directly.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: Financial COMMITMENT for one player's seat in a Session. One Booking per (sessionId, userId).
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a Session seat (Session owns the seat itself). Not a Payment (Payment is a separate aggregate behind PaymentACL). Not the saga.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Class name: Match. WRITE-ONCE. No update methods after MatchCompleted.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: POST-EVENT immutable record of who actually played and for how long.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a Session (which existed before play). Not a Game.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Class name: CellProjection (NOT Cell). NO write methods. Rebuilt from source events.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: Demand cluster projection: (geography, time window, sport, skill band). Recomputed continuously.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not an aggregate. Not authoritative. Not persisted as state of record.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Class name: HostProfile. Methods return capability checks; never mutate coordination.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: User who has been granted hosting capabilities. Provides authority; never orchestrates.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a flag on User. Not a Session method. Not a saga actor.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Karma is referenced ONLY in Gamification. Trust code MUST NOT import KarmaLedger.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: Engagement currency. Awarded for activity. Tracked in KarmaLedger.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a trust signal. Not a reputation score. Not consulted by matchmaking or gating.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: If you write 'reputation' in code, the linter should reject it. Use the specific profile name.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: AMBIGUOUS — REMOVED FROM VOCABULARY. Use one of: SkillProfile, ReliabilityProfile, FinancialTrustProfile, CommunityStandingProfile.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a thing. There is no ReputationProfile in v6.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Trust appears only as a subdomain label. Code uses specific profile types.
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: Adjective only. 'Trust' refers to the four-submodel subdomain. There is no 'Trust' aggregate.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not an aggregate. Not a single number. Not a column on User.

sample_rows.Code Discipline: Method names must specify what's being cancelled. NO polymorphic cancel().
sample_rows.ONE Source-of-Truth Meaning: AMBIGUOUS — context-dependent verb. Use specific verbs: ReleaseSlot, CancelGame, CancelSession, CancelBooking, RequestRefund.
sample_rows.What It Is NOT: Not a generic Cancel() method anywhere.

sample_rows.DB Pattern: INSERT INTO games ... ON CONFLICT (proposer_id, sport, time_window_hash) DO NOTHING
UPDATE games SET status='Open', version=version+1 WHERE game_id=? AND status='Proposed' AND version=?
UPDATE games SET status='Abandoned' WHERE game_id=? AND status IN ('Proposed','Open')
INSERT sessions ... ON CONFLICT (game_id, schedule_hash) DO NOTHING. Requires TimeSlotHeld first via saga.
UPDATE sessions SET held_seats = held_seats + 1, version=version+1 WHERE session_id=? AND held_seats + confirmed_seats < capacity AND version=?
UPDATE sessions SET confirmed_seats = confirmed_seats + 1, held_seats = held_seats - 1 WHERE session_id=? AND version=?
UPDATE sessions SET held_seats = GREATEST(0, held_seats - 1) WHERE session_id=? AND version=?
INSERT bookings ... ON CONFLICT (session_id, user_id) DO NOTHING
UPDATE bookings SET status='Confirmed', payment_id=? WHERE booking_id=? AND status='Created'
UPDATE bookings SET status='Cancelled' WHERE booking_id=? AND status NOT IN ('Cancelled','Refunded')

sample_rows.On Failure: If policy unavailable, default to NoRefund + alert

sample_rows.Action: If FullRefund or PartialRefund: request Financial.Refund(amount)
sample_rows.On Failure: Refund retry with backoff; manual escalation after N
If thresh too tight, skip to step 8

sample_rows.Action: (Replacement Workflow runs in parallel — see below)

sample_rows.On Failure: If no candidates, mark Searching with TTL

sample_rows.Action: Notify top N candidates via NotificationsACL

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: Booking saga step times out (10 min TTL). Payment-Order reconciliation job (every 5 min) detects captured payment without confirmed Booking and either completes the saga or issues compensating refund.
sample_rows.Scenario: PG webhook lost; payment captured but never confirmed

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: Optimistic locking on Session.version + atomic counter. One wins, one gets SeatHoldFailed; loser's Booking saga aborts cleanly.
sample_rows.Scenario: Two users grab last seat in a Session simultaneously

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: Backstop reconciliation detects mismatch. Saga compensation: refund + Session.ReleaseSeat. User notified.
sample_rows.Scenario: Reservation TTL expires during payment processing

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: Saga state persisted before each step. New worker resumes from last persisted state.
Idempotency key on every consumer (see Sheet 2). Second delivery is no-op.
BLOCKED BY DG-4. Coordination has no cancellation logic. No-show is detected by Recovery from MatchStarted.
sample_rows.Scenario: Player no-shows but Coordination 'helpfully' tries to auto-cancel

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: BLOCKED BY DG-1. Schema review rejects. Use the four trust profiles + use-case-bound composition.
sample_rows.Scenario: Developer adds composed_score column to UserProfile for 'performance'

sample_rows.Drift Guard Engaged: DG-2 (Host is capability provider, not orchestrator)
sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: Hosting emits a HostAuthorityGranted(approveJoiners) capability. Coordination's join flow checks `hostingService.canApproveJoiners(hostId)` and admits accordingly. Host NEVER calls Coordination.
sample_rows.Scenario: Feature request: 'Host should approve players before they join'

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: ALLOWED ONLY if cache miss does not change behavior. Performance cache; not correctness store. Otherwise BLOCKED BY DG-1.
sample_rows.Scenario: Trust Composition Policy 'optimization': cache the composed score

sample_rows.Expected v6 Behavior: BNPLDefaulted (Financial) emits to Recovery. CancellationCase opened. Standard cascade: refund (none, since unpaid), Session.ReleaseSeat, ReliabilityObserved penalty, FinancialTrustObserved penalty.

sample_rows.Test Detail: A corporate (Acme Corp) books a recurring weekly Session (badminton, 8 employees, every Tuesday 7-9pm for 12 weeks). Some weeks have late cancellations from individuals. Some weeks have no-shows. Some weeks need replacement players.

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: STOP. Per v6.1 constraint A: no new aggregates unless forced. Model as 12 independent Sessions with a shared metadata tag (corporateBookingId). The recurring nature is metadata, not domain.
sample_rows.Test Detail: 12 separate Sessions (one per week) all linked to a parent CorporateBookingAgreement (NEW concept candidate?)

sample_rows.Drift Guard: DG-2 enforced (Host provides capability, Coordination orchestrates)
sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: Coordinator gets HostQualified + HostAuthorityGranted(create_recurring). They use Coordination's normal Game.Propose flow 12 times. Hosting provides the capability check; Coordination owns the orchestration.
sample_rows.Test Detail: Acme HR designates an internal coordinator. The coordinator user has hosting capability for this Session series.

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: Booking aggregate has userId pointing to the corporate billing user (Acme). One Booking covers all 8 seats. Individual employees get SeatHeld/SeatConfirmed against the Session but no individual Bookings.
sample_rows.Test Detail: Acme pays the venue cost upfront. Individual employees do not pay.
sample_rows.Verdict: PARTIAL PASS — needs explicit modeling decision: is Booking always 1:1 with user-seat, or can one Booking cover N seats? v6 needs to clarify.

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: MatchCompleted with attendedCount=5 < confirmedCount=8. PartialFulfillmentCase opens (Recovery saga). Pricing recalculation produces a refund decision. Refund issued to Acme (the payer).
sample_rows.Test Detail: Week 5: only 5 of 8 confirmed employees show up.

sample_rows.Aspect: Late cancellation by an individual employee
sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: PlayerCancelled event. Recovery opens CancellationCase. Refund eligibility: NoRefund (Acme paid, individual cannot trigger refund). ReplacementCase opens. ReliabilityPenaltyPolicy applies to Employee X individually (not to Acme).
sample_rows.Verdict: PASS — individual reliability tracked separately from corporate financial trust

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: MatchStarted detects missing attendee. NoShowCase opens. ReliabilityObserved applies to Employee Y.
sample_rows.Test Detail: Week 9: Employee Y is confirmed but doesn't show.

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: ReplacementCase opens. Replacement search policy could be scoped to corporate-eligible users (a new policy parameter, not a new aggregate). Booking saga runs for Employee Z's seat — but no new payment because Acme already paid for the Session capacity.
sample_rows.Test Detail: Week 7 after cancellation: Employee Z (also Acme) joins as replacement.
sample_rows.Verdict: PARTIAL PASS — Booking-per-seat model needs refinement: if one corporate Booking covers all seats, replacement is just a SeatConfirmed change with no Booking impact.

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: FinancialTrustProfile updates against Acme's account. ReliabilityProfile updates against individual employees. Trust Composition Policy can be called with use_case='corporate_billing' to evaluate Acme separately from individuals.
sample_rows.Test Detail: Acme has financial trust (paid 12 weeks reliably). Individual employees have reliability trust (some have no-shows).
sample_rows.Verdict: PASS — four trust submodels handle this naturally

sample_rows.How v6 Handles It: (1) Booking aggregate model: clarify whether one Booking can cover N seats or whether it's strictly 1:1 with user-seat. Recommendation: introduce a CorporateBilledBooking variant OR allow Booking.seatCount > 1 with explicit invariant. (2) Replacement search policy needs an eligibility-pool parameter to constrain to corporate users.
sample_rows.Test Detail: v6 mostly handles corporate recurring bookings cleanly. Two refinements needed:
sample_rows.Verdict: REFINEMENTS NEEDED — but no structural changes to v6 cores

sample_rows.Classification: CORE DOMAIN: Synchronous Multi-Player Coordination Under Uncertainty

sample_rows.Entities Within: (Game has no internal entities — small intent aggregate)
sample_rows.Notes: Pre-commitment intent. Mutable until Scheduled. Does NOT own seats or money.
sample_rows.Value Objects: Sport, TimeWindow, Location, SkillRange, Capacity, GameStatus

sample_rows.Notes: Scheduled instance. Owns CAPACITY. Mutable roster, immutable schedule.
sample_rows.Value Objects: SessionStatus, Capacity counters (heldCount, confirmedCount), TimeSlotRef, VenueRef, CourtRef

sample_rows.Notes: Financial commitment for one player's seat. One per (sessionId, userId).
Post-event immutable record. Source of truth for Trust and Recovery.
Owns the deviation lifecycle for any cancellation. One case per cancellation event.
Detected from MatchStarted. Penalty delegated to ReliabilityPenaltyPolicy.
Time-bounded. Searches for replacement after a cancellation.
Atomic counter. PHYSICAL TRUTH ONLY. NEVER list-based.
sample_rows.Value Objects: Capacity, heldCount, confirmedCount, version, TimeWindow, PriceSnapshotRef

header_names: Why It's Not in the Domain Map

sample_rows.Why It's Not in the Domain Map: Stateless function. Not an aggregate. No state to own.
Pure function bound to use_case. NEVER persists a composed score (DG-1).
Pure function. Returns ScoreDelta; does not write.
Pure function. Returns decision; does not execute refund.
Recomputed continuously. Demoted from v5 aggregate.

sample_rows.col1: Playo Domain Model v7 — Essence (consolidated governance)

sample_rows.col2: v7.1 — single-essence governance + per-BC modular sheets. Supersedes v6.1 and v7.0.
Single source of truth for Playo's domain. Each Bounded Context lives in one self-contained sheet with the same internal schema. This sheet holds everything cross-cutting that constrains every BC.
If any BC sheet contradicts a Locked Decision (L#) or Drift Guard (DG#) on this sheet, the L/DG wins. Revise the BC sheet, never the other way.

L# Locked Decision · DG# Drift Guard · VO# Value Object · CMD-<bc>-### Command · AGG-<bc>-### Aggregate · EVT-<bc>-### Event · POL-### Policy · SAG-### Saga · ACL-### Anti-Corruption Layer · RM-### Read Model · INV-<bc>-### Invariant
1) This sheet  2) 05_Domain_Classification  3) 06_Context_Map  4) any BC sheet (09–23)  5) 30_Sagas for cross-BC flows  6) 41_Failure_Scenarios + 42_Pressure_Tests for stress proofs

sample_rows.col1: Value Objects (VO#) — Formal Definitions with Invariants

sample_rows.col3: amount: int (minor units), currency: ISO-4217 (default INR)
sample_rows.col4: amount ≥ 0 (negative amounts require RefundReason wrapper). currency is immutable. Two Money values can only be added/compared if currency matches.
sample_rows.col5: Used in: Booking, Payment, Refund, PriceSnapshot, Settlement, Wallet, BNPLObligation.

sample_rows.col4: endsAt > startsAt. Both stored as UTC. Display TZ is a presentation concern.
sample_rows.col5: Used in: Game, TimeSlot, Session, BNPLObligation, CommissionRate.

sample_rows.col3: lat: decimal(-90..90), lng: decimal(-180..180), radiusM?: int ≥ 0
sample_rows.col5: Used in: Venue, Game (search location), Cell projection.

sample_rows.col4: minBand ≤ maxBand on the SkillBand ordinal.

sample_rows.col3: ordinal enum: Beginner < Intermediate < Advanced < Pro
sample_rows.col4: Closed enum. New bands require a Locked Decision.
sample_rows.col5: Used in: SkillRange, SkillProfile (derived), Cell projection.

sample_rows.col4: min ≤ max. Capacity is set at Game.Propose and immutable thereafter.
Required on every Command (DG-7). Uniqueness enforced per (aggregate, operation).
sample_rows.col5: Used in: every Command DTO, every external integration callback.
The differentiating problem. Battle-tested with 4 Drift Guards.
Necessary substrate for Coordination but not the differentiator.
Four independent profiles composed only by Trust Composition Policy under L4.
Contractual truth (L6). Distinct from Inventory (physical truth).

sample_rows.col4: Coordination requests PriceSnapshot.Seal at Session scheduling time.
Coordination publishes BookingCreated; Financial authorises/captures Payment; Coordination consumes PaymentCaptured to confirm Booking.
Coordination publishes DeviationRequested; Recovery owns all deviation events (L10).
Recovery emits *Observed events; Trust profiles consume.
Pure identity. Owns User. Behind IdentityACL. NO preferences, NO profile attributes (those are projections in Community).

sample_rows.col5: phone unique. authProvider unique. Idempotent on phone OR authProvider.
User must exist. authProvider not already linked to another User.

sample_rows.col4: Owns: userId, phone:Phone, authProviders:[AuthProviderRef], status, createdAt, deactivatedAt?
sample_rows.col5: phone immutable post-creation (change requires re-verification command, future scope). Status monotonic: Active → Deactivated. authProviders append-only.

sample_rows.col4: Auth session (NOT a coordination Session). Token, expiry. Behind IdentityACL.
Core differentiator. Owns Game (intent), Session (capacity), Booking (financial commitment, 1:1 L9), Match (post-event truth). Does NOT own deviations (L1, DG-4).
proposerId(User), sport:Sport, timeWindow:TimeWindow, location:Geo, skillRange:SkillRange, capacity:Capacity, idempotencyKey
sample_rows.col5: proposer must be Active User. Capacity.min ≤ Capacity.max. timeWindow in future.
Game in Proposed. host has CreateGame capability OR is the proposer.

sample_rows.col4: gameId, venueId, courtId, timeSlotId, scheduledTimeWindow:TimeWindow, idempotencyKey

sample_rows.col7: On Failure
If hold fails → emit GameDeviationRequested (Recovery → GameAbandoned).
If seal fails → release TimeSlot + emit GameDeviationRequested.
Idempotent retry.
Emits SessionScheduled.
Unique on (sessionId, userId).
PG idempotent.
On failure: PaymentDeviationRequested → Recovery → BookingCancelled + SeatReleased.
Forbidden Behavior

sample_rows.col4: Cell projection (recomputed), historical fill rates, capital budget
sample_rows.col7: MUST NOT call PG. MUST NOT mutate aggregates. Emits SubsidyDecisionMade fact only.
MUST NOT manipulate trust signals. MUST NOT push notifications directly (delegated to NotificationsACL).

sample_rows.col3: Per use case: matchmaking | gating | display | dispute resolution
sample_rows.col7: MUST NOT compute a generic 'reputation score'. MUST bind to use_case (L4, DG-1). Different use cases get different compositions.
MUST NOT directly write to ReliabilityProfile — emits an observation.

sample_rows.col4: BookingCancelled | SessionCancelled, cancellation timing, reason, FinancialTrust profile, Venue policy
sample_rows.col7: MUST NOT execute refund. Emits a RefundDecided; Financial executes.
MUST NOT influence Trust composition. Karma stays in Gamification.

sample_rows.col4: TimeSlot, time-of-day, demand signals, applicable discounts, GST tier
sample_rows.col7: MUST be deterministic given inputs. MUST NOT seal snapshots itself — emits computed breakdown.

sample_rows.col5: External order/intent ↔ internal Payment; internal Payment ↔ external transaction

Direct provider calls from Coordination, Recovery, or any Policy.

PMS-specific data structures from polluting Inventory.

Predictive model output. Versioned model, not state.

sample_rows.col4: Cached view for UI display (per use_case). NEVER a write-side aggregate.
sample_rows.col5: Cache. Authoritative state lives in 4 trust profiles. Cache miss MUST NOT change behavior (DG-1).
KarmaLedger is authoritative; ranking is derived.

sample_rows.col2: Each BC sheet contains an §IDEMPOTENCY section with its operations.

sample_rows.col3: Creating new aggregate roots where natural-key collision must be prevented.

sample_rows.col4: UPDATE ... WHERE id=? AND version=? RETURNING version+1.
UPDATE ... SET counter=counter+1 WHERE counter+other ≤ capacity.
INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; subsequent updates rejected.

sample_rows.col3: Trust profile observations, Wallet transactions, KarmaLedger.
sample_rows.col4: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (entity, sourceEventId) DO NOTHING.

sample_rows.col2: PG webhook lost; payment captured but never confirmed
sample_rows.col3: Booking saga TTL (10 min). Payment-Booking reconciliation job (every 5 min) detects mismatch, issues compensating refund OR completes the saga.

sample_rows.col2: Two users grab last seat in a Session simultaneously
sample_rows.col3: Optimistic locking on Session.version + atomic counter (heldCount + confirmedCount ≤ capacity). One wins, one gets SeatHoldFailed.

sample_rows.col2: Reservation TTL expires during payment processing
sample_rows.col3: Backstop reconciliation detects mismatch. Saga compensation: refund + ReleaseSeat.
Saga state persisted before each step. New worker resumes from last persisted step.
Idempotency key on every consumer (DG-7 + per-BC §IDEMPOTENCY). Second delivery is no-op.

sample_rows.col2: Player no-shows but Coordination 'helpfully' tries to auto-cancel
sample_rows.col3: BLOCKED BY DG-4 + DG-5. Coordination has no cancellation logic. No-show detected by Recovery (SAG-007).

sample_rows.col2: Developer adds composed_score column to UserProfile
sample_rows.col3: BLOCKED BY DG-1. Schema review rejects.
Acme Corp books a recurring weekly Session (badminton, 8 employees, 12 weeks).
sample_rows.col4: 12 Sessions × 8 Bookings = 96 independent Bookings (L9). All linked via CorporateBookingAgreement (Financial BC) for invoicing. Coordinator user has HostProfile with CreateRecurring capability (DG-2). Acme's FinancialTrustProfile updated by aggregate of payments. Each employee's ReliabilityProfile updated independently.
Replacement Search Policy applies L14 filter. If no candidate within TTL → ReplacementFailed → PartialFulfillmentCase opens after MatchCompleted; partial refund issued.
VenueUnavailable → SAG-006. Full refunds issued (override eligibility). Sessions cancelled by Recovery. TimeSlots released.

sample_rows.col3: User defaults on BNPL, has 3 future bookings funded by it.

sample_rows.col2: Two ACLs added for the same external system (e.g., a new PG)
sample_rows.col3: Add a new PG implementation behind PaymentACL; no other BC changes.
sample_rows.col4: PASS — ACL-01 is the only seam.
VO-14 enum is closed; requires a new Locked Decision and a value-object update. All BCs that consume Sport import from VO-14 (single source).
PriceRule v2 published; new PriceSnapshots reflect surge; existing snapshots immutable. No changes to Booking, Session, Inventory.

sample_rows.col3: Each bounded context is a single self-contained sheet with identical schema (§META → §COMMANDS → §AGGREGATES → §ENTITIES → §VALUE OBJECTS → §STATE MACHINE → §EVENTS → §POLICIES USED → §IDEMPOTENCY → §BOUNDARY/ACL → §INVARIANTS).
sample_rows.col4: Adds 15 BC sheets; each can be added/edited/audited in isolation.

sample_rows.col3: Defines User aggregate, AuthSession entity, Phone/AuthProviderRef VOs, lifecycle, invariants.
Defines Payment, Refund, SplitPaymentGroup, BNPLObligation, Wallet, CorporateBookingAgreement. Single money-state context (L11).
sample_rows.col4: Closes 'Financial bounded context not defined' gap.

sample_rows.col3: Defines PriceRule, PriceSnapshot, CommissionRate aggregates with full lifecycle and invariants.
sample_rows.col4: Closes 'Pricing implied but not defined' gap.

sample_rows.col3: Adds Settlement aggregate; full PartnerRelationship lifecycle, commission versioning, KYC gating.
Each Trust submodel is a full BC with commands, aggregates, events, idempotency, invariants. Bayesian skill, reliability score, financial trust, community standing.

sample_rows.col1: Playo Domain Model v7 — README & Navigation

sample_rows.col2: v7.0 — modular bounded-context model. Supersedes v6.1.

A single source of truth for Playo's domain. Each bounded context is a self-contained sheet with identical internal schema. Cross-cutting concerns (locks, drift guards, value objects, sagas, ACLs) live in dedicated sheets and are referenced by ID.
1) 01_Locked_Decisions  2) 02_Drift_Guards  3) 03_Ubiquitous_Language  4) 06_Context_Map  5) Pick a BC sheet (10–24)  6) 30_Sagas to see how BCs interact.

Every BC sheet has the same row-section structure: §META → §COMMANDS → §AGGREGATES → §ENTITIES → §VALUE OBJECTS → §STATE MACHINE → §EVENTS EMITTED → §EVENTS CONSUMED → §POLICIES USED → §IDEMPOTENCY → §BOUNDARY/ACL → §INVARIANTS (formal).

See 43_Changelog. Key: Financial, Identity, Pricing fully modeled; 4 Trust profiles defined with invariants & operations; Commands introduced; Value Objects formalised; Booking cardinality locked (L9); SessionCancelled re-homed to Recovery (L12); 3 new Drift Guards.

sample_rows.col1: How to add a new BC
sample_rows.col2: Copy any BC sheet, rename to '2N_BC_<Name>', fill the same sections. Add an entry in 05_Domain_Classification and 06_Context_Map. Reference cross-cutting items by ID, never duplicate.
L# = Locked Decision; DG# = Drift Guard; VO# = Value Object; CMD-<bc>-### = Command; AGG-<bc>-### = Aggregate; EVT-<bc>-### = Event; POL-### = Policy; ACL-### = ACL; SAG-### = Saga.
Recovery owns ALL deviations from intended coordination outcome.
sample_rows.col3: Resolves Coord vs Recovery boundary. Failure events ALWAYS belong to Recovery regardless of where they originate.

sample_rows.col2: Session owns capacity (counters). Booking owns financial commitment. Seat ALLOCATION (held/release) MUST NOT depend synchronously on payment finalisation.
sample_rows.col3: Prevents deadlocks. Restated in v7: 'allocation' = capacity-counter mutation; 'confirmation' = the seat is paid. Confirmation MAY depend on PaymentCaptured.
sample_rows.col4: Tightened in v7 (was ambiguous in v6 — see Changelog)

sample_rows.col2: Policies are stateless, do not orchestrate, do not call external systems, only emit decisions.
sample_rows.col3: Prevents Policy Layer from becoming a god layer.

sample_rows.col2: TrustScore = f(use_case, skill, reliability, financial, community). Use-case binding mandatory.
Host PROVIDES capabilities. Coordination OWNS game assembly. Host is never an orchestrator.
sample_rows.col3: Prevents Host from becoming a hidden Coordination layer.

sample_rows.col2: Inventory = physical truth. Partner Relations = contractual truth. Availability never mixed.
sample_rows.col3: Two contexts, two kinds of truth.

sample_rows.col2: Build order: Events → Aggregates → Policies → Workflows. NEVER the reverse.

sample_rows.col3: Trust submodel separation. Prevents 4 trust profiles from being collapsed into a single 'reputation'.
sample_rows.col4: (a) NO persisted composed_score column anywhere. (b) NO method named getReputation/getTrustScore without use_case. (c) Trust Composition Policy is a pure function bound to use_case (L4).
sample_rows.col5: Linter rejects 'reputation' identifier; schema review rejects composed_score columns; method signatures lacking use_case parameter.

sample_rows.col3: Hosting as capability provider only. Prevents Host from becoming a hidden Coordination layer.
sample_rows.col4: (a) HostProfile emits ONLY capability events. (b) HostProfile methods take no Session/Game/Booking arguments. (c) Coordination CONSULTS host capability checks but never delegates orchestration.
sample_rows.col5: Method signatures like host.approveJoinerForSession(session, user); HostProfile imports Session/Booking types.

sample_rows.col3: Policy non-aggregation. Prevents Policy Layer from becoming a god layer.
sample_rows.col4: (a) Policies MUST NOT call external systems. (b) Policies MUST NOT mutate state. (c) Policies return decisions; orchestrators apply them.
sample_rows.col5: Policy class with injected repository or HTTP client; policy method with void return.

sample_rows.col4: (a) Cancelled/Failed/NoShowed events live in Recovery. (b) Coordination/Financial publish only DeviationRequested(reason); Recovery converts. (c) Coordination aggregates have no cancellation_reason field.
sample_rows.col5: Coordination aggregate with cancellation_reason field; Coordination method named cancel(); event class *Cancelled in non-Recovery package.

sample_rows.col3: Aggregates emit only INTENDED-LIFECYCLE events. They do not emit their own failure events.
sample_rows.col4: (a) Booking does not emit BookingCancelled — it emits BookingDeviationRequested; Recovery emits BookingCancelled after opening a CancellationCase. (b) Same for Session, Payment.
sample_rows.col5: An aggregate emitting an event whose name ends in Cancelled/Failed/NoShowed/Defaulted.

sample_rows.col3: Value objects are immutable, validated on construction, side-effect free.
sample_rows.col4: (a) No setters on any class in 04_Value_Objects. (b) Construction throws if invariants violated. (c) Equality is structural.
sample_rows.col5: Setter on Money/TimeWindow/Geo; mutating methods like Money.add() that change in place.

sample_rows.col3: Aggregates receive commands, not arbitrary calls.
sample_rows.col4: (a) Every public aggregate method maps 1:1 to a Command DTO. (b) Command DTOs carry actorId, intent, idempotencyKey. (c) Aggregates reject commands missing the idempotencyKey.
sample_rows.col5: Public aggregate method without a corresponding Command class; service code calling aggregate.update(...) without a Command.

sample_rows.col1: Ubiquitous Language — Single Source of Truth

sample_rows.col2: Pre-commitment INTENT to play. Sport, time window, location, skill range, capacity.
sample_rows.col3: Not a Session. Not a Booking. Not a Match.

sample_rows.col2: Scheduled INSTANCE of a Game (venue + time slot locked). Owns CAPACITY via heldCount/confirmedCount.
sample_rows.col3: Not a Game. Not a Booking. Not a Match.

sample_rows.col2: Financial COMMITMENT for ONE player's ONE seat in a Session. 1:1 with seat (L9).
sample_rows.col3: Not a Session seat. Not a Payment. Not a group container.

sample_rows.col2: POST-EVENT immutable record of who actually played and for how long.
Position within a Session. Lifecycle Held → Confirmed → Released. Modeled as counters + audit log.
sample_rows.col3: Not a TimeSlot. Not a Booking.

sample_rows.col2: Bookable atomic time window on a Court at a Venue. Counter-based inventory unit.
Demand cluster PROJECTION (geo, time, sport, skill band). Recomputed continuously.

sample_rows.col1: Value Objects (VO#) — Formal Definitions with Invariants

sample_rows.col3: amount: int (minor units), currency: ISO-4217 (default INR)
sample_rows.col4: amount ≥ 0 (negative amounts require RefundReason wrapper). currency is immutable. Two Money values can only be added/compared if currency matches.
sample_rows.col5: Used in: Booking, Payment, Refund, PriceSnapshot, Settlement, Wallet, BNPLObligation.

sample_rows.col4: endsAt > startsAt. Both stored as UTC. Display TZ is a presentation concern.
sample_rows.col5: Used in: Game, TimeSlot, Session, BNPLObligation, CommissionRate.

sample_rows.col3: lat: decimal(-90..90), lng: decimal(-180..180), radiusM?: int ≥ 0
sample_rows.col5: Used in: Venue, Game (search location), Cell projection.

sample_rows.col4: minBand ≤ maxBand on the SkillBand ordinal.

sample_rows.col3: ordinal enum: Beginner < Intermediate < Advanced < Pro
sample_rows.col4: Closed enum. New bands require a Locked Decision.
sample_rows.col5: Used in: SkillRange, SkillProfile (derived), Cell projection.

sample_rows.col4: min ≤ max. Capacity is set at Game.Propose and immutable thereafter.
Required on every Command (DG-7). Uniqueness enforced per (aggregate, operation).
sample_rows.col5: Used in: every Command DTO, every external integration callback.
The differentiating problem. Battle-tested with 4 Drift Guards.
Necessary substrate for Coordination but not the differentiator.

sample_rows.col3: Booking saga TTL (10 min). Payment-Booking reconciliation job (every 5 min) detects mismatch, issues compensating refund OR completes the saga.

sample_rows.col2: Two users grab last seat in a Session simultaneously
sample_rows.col3: Optimistic locking on Session.version + atomic counter (heldCount + confirmedCount ≤ capacity). One wins, one gets SeatHoldFailed.

sample_rows.col2: Reservation TTL expires during payment processing
sample_rows.col3: Backstop reconciliation detects mismatch. Saga compensation: refund + ReleaseSeat.
Saga state persisted before each step. New worker resumes from last persisted step.
Idempotency key on every consumer (DG-7 + per-BC §IDEMPOTENCY). Second delivery is no-op.

Acme Corp books a recurring weekly Session (badminton, 8 employees, 12 weeks).
sample_rows.col4: 12 Sessions × 8 Bookings = 96 independent Bookings (L9). All linked via CorporateBookingAgreement (Financial BC) for invoicing. Coordinator user has HostProfile with CreateRecurring capability (DG-2). Acme's FinancialTrustProfile updated by aggregate of payments. Each employee's ReliabilityProfile updated independently.
Replacement Search Policy applies L14 filter. If no candidate within TTL → ReplacementFailed → PartialFulfillmentCase opens after MatchCompleted; partial refund issued.
VenueUnavailable → SAG-006. Full refunds issued (override eligibility). Sessions cancelled by Recovery. TimeSlots released.

sample_rows.col3: User defaults on BNPL, has 3 future bookings funded by it.
